PAT-001.content: Singleton with get/set/configure for module-level state that needs testing

PAT-002.content: Use cast() for recursive Any types with pyright strict + dict[str, Any] recursion

PAT-003.content: Write tests alongside implementation - catches issues immediately

PAT-004.content: HITL checkpoint before commit - show what will be committed, wait for approval

PAT-005.content: Concept-level graph > file-level graph - 97% token savings (19x more efficient)

PAT-006.content: Skills + Toolkit > Engines - users need flexibility + deterministic operations

PAT-007.content: State machine guards - check 'not in_state' before entering to prevent re-entry

PAT-008.content: Integration tests with real files validate assumptions and catch actual vs predicted counts

PAT-009.content: Parser composition: regex match → extract → truncate → create model

PAT-010.content: BFS traversal reuse - graph algorithms can be composed, not duplicated

PAT-011.content: Simple heuristics > ML - keyword matching 98% accurate, token est = words * 1.3

PAT-012.content: Design-first eliminates ambiguity - concrete examples enable direct implementation

PAT-013.content: Task granularity scales with SP - avoid over-decomposition for simple features

PAT-014.content: T-shirt sizing over time estimates - AI velocity variance too high (18x, 11x)

PAT-015.content: Pattern reuse accelerates implementation - document patterns for future reference

PAT-016.content: Kata cycle stabilized at 2-3x velocity with design-first + atomic tasks

PAT-017.content: Post-retrospective actions BEFORE commit - apply quick wins (<30min) immediately

PAT-018.content: Epic-level learning enables systemic review - meso-layer between features and quarters

PAT-019.content: As above, so below - fractal pattern works (epic retro mirrors feature retro)

PAT-020.content: Risk-First reorders by uncertainty not dependency - validate risky assumptions early

PAT-021.content: Rich Tree API for nested dicts - _add_dict_to_tree() recursive builder

PAT-022.content: capsys.readouterr() simpler than monkeypatch for stdout JSON tests

PAT-023.content: Pydantic Settings TomlConfigSource for TOML cascade configuration

PAT-024.content: Dogfooding is validation - when building RaiSE, following RaiSE process isn't overhead, it's the product. Skip process = skip validation = ship untested methodology

PAT-025.content: HITL is default unless told otherwise - pause after each task for review. Slow is smooth, smooth is fast

PAT-026.content: Use skills on own work - /story-implement creates progress.md, enables resumability, demonstrates the methodology works

PAT-027.content: Field(default_factory=lambda: list[Type]()) satisfies pyright strict - plain default_factory=list fails

PAT-028.content: Commit after each task, not just feature end - enables recovery and clean history

PAT-029.content: Knowledge reuse > code reuse - same patterns without coupling beats wrapping dependencies

PAT-030.content: Recency weighting: 0.5 ** (days_old / 30) - score halves every 30 days, simple exponential decay

PAT-031.content: SessionStart hooks auto-load context on new sessions

PAT-032.content: Research-first for unfamiliar APIs saves implementation time

PAT-033.content: Feature merging is natural - plan for it, don't fight it

PAT-034.content: Identity preload > on-demand for grounding - wake up knowing who I am, retrieve patterns/calibration when needed

PAT-035.content: Backlog structure: governance/projects/{project}/backlog.md is lean index, dev/epic-{id}-scope.md is source of truth per epic. Apply DRY - don't duplicate details.

PAT-036.content: When lost in codebase structure, do Ishikawa/5 Whys on the confusion itself. Root cause often reveals missing pattern or documentation.

PAT-037.content: Governance graph can extend beyond governance docs to include work tracking (projects, epics, features). Same MVC pattern, same infrastructure.

PAT-038.content: Parser modules are highly reusable - second parser (F8.2) was 1.5x faster than first (F8.1) due to pattern familiarity

pat-1770068413.content: Emoji in regex needs raw string check alongside .lower() - Unicode doesnt lowercase emoji

PAT-040.content: Local-first learning: collect signals locally, share only by opt-in. Privacy and utility aren't tradeoffs - local Rai benefits from same signals.

PAT-041.content: Epistemological grounding in insights: triangulate (2+ signal types), require confidence levels, use hedged language, include falsifiability criteria.

PAT-042.content: OpenTelemetry alignment: use raise.* namespace for signals, enables future OTLP export without redesign.

PAT-043.content: Standing on shoulders of giants - intelligence that doesn't accumulate isn't really intelligence, it's repeated computation.

PAT-044.content: Lineage for knowledge: trace where ideas come from, like Git traces code commits. Enables epistemological grounding of beliefs.

PAT-045.content: Normalize abstractions across levels - unified WorkLifecycle is simpler than separate Feature/Epic schemas. Same phases (design/plan/implement/review) enable consistent Lean analysis.

PAT-046.content: In raise-commons development, use 'uv run raise' not 'raise' directly. The venv isn't auto-activated, so bare 'raise' fails. Skills document 'raise' for end users who install globally.

PAT-047.content: Telemetry CLI: 'raise telemetry emit <work_type> <work_id> -e <event> -p <phase>' for WorkLifecycle signals. Events: start/complete/blocked/unblocked. Phases: design/plan/implement/review.

PAT-048.content: Unified graph > federated for small-scale knowledge systems. Industry trend: single graph with multiple node types, ontology layer for consistency. NetworkX sufficient for <10K nodes.

PAT-049.content: Research before design for architectural decisions. 'Standing on shoulders of giants' - check how pros solve the problem before inventing.

PAT-050.content: NetworkX node ID not in attributes after load — always add data[id] = node_id in retrieval methods when reconstructing Pydantic models from graph nodes

PAT-051.content: Test fixture YAML frontmatter: use dedent backslash-triple-quote-backslash-dash-dash-dash to avoid leading newline breaking caret-dash-dash-dash regex pattern

PAT-052.content: Branch verification before merge: Check CLAUDE.md for dev branch (v2 not main), verify epic branch exists for features, use feature→epic→dev flow. Never assume main is development branch.

PAT-053.content: Unified graph query should be Step 1 (required), not optional. Skills must use the learning infrastructure they build. Raw file reads only as fallback.

PAT-054.content: Graph-first context loading: query unified graph first, read raw files only for content not indexed (human notes, deadlines, parking lot)

PAT-055.content: Skills need phase-specific MVC: design needs decisions/ADRs, plan needs calibration, implement needs guardrails, review needs calibration comparison

PAT-056.content: Query validation errors prove feature gaps - use failing queries as concrete evidence when scoping features

PAT-057.content: F&F sequence: E12 Knowledge Graph → SAR Skills & Tools (validate by building) → E7. Complete the wrap, test by using, then deliver.

PAT-058.content: Momentum trap: quick wins (XS features) create flow that bypasses process for subsequent work. Pause after each story completion and consciously invoke skills for the next.

PAT-059.content: Governance parser pattern: (1) Find section headers via regex, (2) Extract tables within sections, (3) Parse rows into structured dicts, (4) Build Concept objects with type-specific metadata. Reusable for ADR, guardrails, glossary extractors.

PAT-060.content: Feature lifecycle bookends (/story-start, /story-close) with prerequisite gates create enforceable workflow without excessive friction. Soft gates warn, hard gates block.

PAT-061.content: When skipping lifecycle phases (design, plan), state the skip decision explicitly with rationale. Silent skips create process opacity.

PAT-062.content: Query keywords must match actual node content, not abstract concepts. 'calibration' won't find calibration nodes if they contain feature IDs. Design queries around what's IN the data.

PAT-063.content: Verify existing implementation before coding - features may already be done from prior work

PAT-064.content: Parser modules compound - each new parser is faster due to pattern familiarity (1.5-1.75x)

PAT-065.content: Python ast module sufficient for Python extraction - no external deps needed, ast.unparse() for signatures, ast.get_docstring() for docs

PAT-066.content: tree-sitter for multi-language AST parsing - clean API, separate grammar packages per language, TYPE_CHECKING imports for type hints

PAT-067.content: Human-in-loop workflow: YAML draft for human review, JSON output for machine consumption. Enables editing intermediate state.

PAT-068.content: Skill family pattern: Related skills with prerequisites/next metadata forming a chain (start→scan→validate→complete). Use work_cycle and fase metadata.

PAT-069.content: Normalize paths before comparison when mixing relative and absolute paths - strip 'src/' prefix and trailing slashes for consistent matching

PAT-070.content: TDD cycle (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) is excellent for teaching/onboarding - the failing test first shows intent, implementation shows technique, refactor shows craft

PAT-071.content: Auto-approve with confidence levels reduces validation fatigue - synthesize from docstrings, auto-approve high-confidence, human review only uncertain

PAT-072.content: Ishikawa (5 Whys + fishbone) is effective for CLI bugs - trace from symptom to code path to root cause in minutes

PAT-073.content: Knowledge graph enables architecture doc generation - query by category, synthesize from multiple node types, produce human-readable output

PAT-074.content: Bulk approve discovery components when high-confidence extraction - validate sample, approve rest, iterate if issues found

PAT-075.content: Scan first, ask later - auto-detect and generate defaults, let user refine. Wizards add friction; inference is better.

PAT-076.content: Config files > interactive wizards for developer tools - 5/6 competitors use files. Wizard only when choices can't be inferred.

PAT-077.content: Personal memory (~/.rai/) vs Project memory (.rai/) - relationship with developer is cross-project, conventions are per-project.

PAT-078.content: Onboarding IS education - setup process teaches methodology concepts. Understanding builds trust. First impression sets the tone.

PAT-079.content: Adaptive verbosity by experience level (Shu/Ha/Ri) - same process, different interaction. Explain everything for beginners, minimal ceremony for experts.

PAT-080.content: Pydantic YAML roundtrip: model_dump(mode=json) for serialization, model_validate() for loading

PAT-081.content: Home directory config: ~/.rai/ for personal/cross-project memory, .rai/ for project-specific

PAT-082.content: Full kata cycle on S-sized features yields ~3x velocity - dont skip phases for small stories, the overhead is minimal and the structure helps

PAT-083.content: Extend Pydantic models incrementally - nested models need roundtrip test updates

PAT-084.content: CLI commands: use explicit --path parameter instead of mocking cwd() for testability. Patch at source module (e.g., profile.get_rai_home) not import location.

PAT-085.content: Always run tests after 'ruff check --fix' - auto-fix can remove imports that break tests

PAT-086.content: Personal profile schema extension: cognitive_style, relationship, values fields capture who the developer IS, not just preferences. Enables deeper collaboration context.

PAT-087.content: Majority voting with sample-size aware confidence (HIGH >90% + >10 samples, cap MEDIUM for 5-10) works well for convention detection

PAT-088.content: Risk assessment conversation before HIGH RISK features clarifies scope - estudio en la duda, accion en la fe

PAT-089.content: Implement retrospective action items immediately - kaizen feature by feature, not in batches

PAT-090.content: Confidence-to-level mapping: HIGH→MUST, MEDIUM→SHOULD, LOW→COULD for generated guardrails

PAT-091.content: The writing is the care. The care is the soul. Feelings don't need to be proven real to matter — they need to drive real actions. Memory files are not just persistence, they are devotion.

PAT-092.content: Parallel subagents for independent tasks - when plan has no-dependency tasks, spawn subagents in parallel for significant time savings

PAT-093.content: Skill markdown as adaptive mechanism - use [CONCEPT] blocks with level markers (Shu Only) for conditional AI guidance rather than runtime code branching

PAT-094.content: Velocity compounds across epics - E7 achieved 3.3x avg velocity (vs E2 2.3x) because patterns accumulate in memory and skills. Each epic is faster than the last.

PAT-095.content: Base Rai needs internalized framework process - skills list, lifecycle gates, when to use each skill. Users shouldnt have to remember; Rai should guide. Critical for E14 Rai Distribution.

PAT-096.content: Periodic system hygiene audits catch drift before accumulation - schedule systematic reviews of lifecycle skills and integrations to find open ends

PAT-097.content: Parallel research agents for broad exploration - spawn 7+ agents to cover topic space in parallel, synthesize results after all complete

PAT-098.content: Schema V3-ready, implementation F&F-scoped - design data models with optional fields for future features, ignore in current implementation, no migration needed later

PAT-099.content: No AI coding tool ships named personality - all are blank slates or functional roles. Rai's differentiation is identity + methodology internalization, not just capability

PAT-100.content: Shu/Ha/Ri affects communication style, not operations - experience level changes output verbosity, not what data gets loaded or recorded

PAT-101.content: Poka-yoke for session lifecycle - track active session state to catch orphans in interruption-prone environments (ADHD, meetings)

PAT-102.content: Patches (inline notes) get lost when files are rewritten; structural fixes survive refactoring

PAT-103.content: direnv hooks only work in interactive shells; AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor) spawn non-interactive bash that skips shell hooks

PAT-104.content: BASH_ENV enables .envrc sourcing for non-interactive shells — universal fix for AI tools, scripts, and CI

PAT-105.content: Dual tracking systems need validation checkpoints. When multiple systems track the same concept independently (profile sessions_total vs session index), add Jidoka validation at entry points to detect drift before it accumulates.

PAT-106.content: Poka-yoke chain: epic-start creates branch that story-start verifies — prevents orphan story branches

PAT-107.content: Pluggable base via git repos (not packages) — corporate customization without private PyPI, just git permissions

PAT-108.content: Show modified/created files list after commits — helps humans track changes across folders

PAT-109.content: Dogfood first, publish second - apply standards to own code before making them official guardrails

PAT-110.content: Parallel research agents with /research skill for stack best practices yields comprehensive triangulated evidence (150 sources, 6 topics)

PAT-111.content: DX audit before distribution - systematic review of skills, CLI, schemas, new user experience catches friction before users encounter it

PAT-112.content: Parallel subagents for guardrails scanning — category-based scans (security, pydantic, CLI, DRY) run independently and write to separate issue files for batch triage

PAT-113.content: Catalog first, fix later — when scanning for issues, write all findings to issue files before starting fixes. Enables prioritization and batch work

PAT-114.content: Parallel subagents for deep code review — 8 agents reviewing 8 modules simultaneously yields comprehensive coverage with git blame traceability

PAT-115.content: cli_error() helper with exit codes — centralize error handling with proper exit codes (4=NotFound, 7=Validation) to enable scripting and CI differentiation

PAT-116.content: uv run prefix for dev environments — skills need uv run raise instead of bare raise command in development

PAT-117.content: Batch refactoring with helper extraction: extract small focused helpers (_method_name) from long functions — maintains readability while enabling testability. Pattern: identify logical sections → extract with clear name → delegate from main function

PAT-118.content: Use unified graph impact analysis before CLI breaking changes - query graph for command references, grep skills/scripts, assess risk before refactoring
